name: Ptysie (Stuffed Swirls)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Grease and lightly flour a large baking sheet.
3. In a saucepan, combine water, 1/2 cup butter/margarine, and salt. Bring to boil over medium heat.
4. Add flour all at once. Stir vigorously with a wooden spoon until smooth and dough forms a ball that leaves the side of the pan.
5. Remove from heat. Add eggs, one at a time, stirring until smooth after each addition.
6. Fit a large pastry bag with a fluted nozzle. Pipe dollops of dough onto the prepared baking sheet, leaving room for spreading.
7. Bake 20-25 minutes until golden brown. Let cool.
8. Melt 2 tablespoons butter/margarine in a large skillet. Add onions and mushrooms; sauté over medium-low heat until tender.
9. Stir sour cream into onion mixture; let cool.
10. Cut baked pastry swirls horizontally in half. Using a spoon or small paring knife, remove any soft dough inside the puffs, leaving a hollow shell about 1/4 to 3/8 inch thick.
11. Spoon about 1 tablespoon of sour cream mixture into the bottom portion of each swirl.
12. Replace the top portion of the swirls.
13. Preheat oven to 350°F. Bake on a large baking sheet for 10 minutes or until heated through.
